description_long: This object is the core of an apple that has been mostly eaten, with only the central fibrous core and a small amount of flesh remaining. The surface is irregular and uneven, with visible bite marks and indentations along the sides. The top retains a small portion of the reddish apple skin, while the rest of the surface is a pale, yellowish-white color with some brown discoloration, likely from oxidation. The core appears slightly shriveled and moist, with fibrous textures and some translucent areas. There are no visible seeds, but the central cavity is apparent. The object looks discarded and is typical of food waste found in urban environments.
description: A mostly eaten apple core with reddish skin at the top, pale fibrous flesh, and visible bite marks.
description_view_0: The apple core is seen with a prominent bite mark on the left and a small patch of reddish skin at the top.
description_view_1: This side shows more of the pale, fibrous core with a deep indentation and less visible skin.
description_view_2: The front displays the central cavity and the most remaining reddish skin, with pronounced bite marks.
description_view_3: The back view reveals a large, dark cavity and the least amount of skin, emphasizing the eaten, hollowed-out core.
attribute_visible_damage_or_blemishes: brown spots, bite marks, shriveled areas
